action.reasoning: Observation: v6 heuristic still poor (max safe=71/500, avg~17, success=0, found_rate~0, avg_dist~5.5m, low detector usage, 1 collision in 5ep). Config fully inspected (max_steps=750, threshold=500, success_angle=0.2618rad/15deg, face_threshold=0.5). Analysis: aggressive seeking insufficient to catch random-walking oracle humanoid; OA logic suboptimal (wrong turn bias); no deadzone leads to oscillation; human speed ~0.3-0.5m/s not matched in follow mode. Root cause is myopic reactive controller cannot maintain lock on dynamic target without better path planning or tuned gains/speed matching. Literature SPF heuristic better but complex to integrate now. Decision: update to v7 heuristic (more aggressive seek linear=1.0 if dist>2.5 + stronger angular=-5*angle, better OA with left/right depth comparison to turn to open side, deadzone, speed matching linear=0.35 when in [1,2)m+good angle, backup on <0.8m); complete full non-truncated predict.py + test_full_episode.py with v7 + expanded diagnostics (per-epi details, left/right depth); update train.py version note; run test_full_episode.py to measure improvement before submit_eval.
